_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn when and how to refine criteria to improve clarity and support analysis and evaluation, including of competing claims.

_source.w: We learn when and how to refine criteria to improve clarity and support analysis and evaluation, including of competing claims.
y: Criteria refinement demonstrates the highest level of evaluative thinking — being critical of our own standards.
Well-refined criteria enable more nuanced, precise and defensible analysis.
This iterative approach to criteria development reflects how experts in every field work.
eg: We refine evaluation criteria based on our developing understanding of the issue.
We adjust criteria when we discover they are too vague, biased or incomplete.
We use refined criteria to analyse competing claims with greater precision.
We discuss how criteria refinement can change the outcome of an evaluation.
We present our criteria development process — initial criteria, refinements, justifications.
We evaluate the quality of criteria themselves — are they clear, relevant, measurable and fair?
